---
title: "2026 AAG Symposium on GIScience for Human Experience and Responsibility (GISER)"
---

# 2026 AAG Symposium on GIScience for Human Experience and Responsibility (GISER)

Recent advances in GIScience—including geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI), emerging sensing technologies, and large-scale geospatial data—are rapidly transforming how we study **human experience of place** and **human–environment relationships**. Researchers increasingly leverage geotagged texts and images to map emotion and place identity, use street-view imagery to model built environments and perceived safety, and develop new GeoAI methods that support human creativity in map-making and urban design. Collectively, this work highlights a shift toward a more **human-centered, humanistic GIScience** that contextualizes spatial activity with subjective experience, cultural meaning, and social context.

At the same time, the ethical implications of these technologies have become impossible to ignore: geoprivacy risks, bias amplification, limited transparency of black-box models, and uncertainty in AI-generated answers. Integrating **responsible thinking** into GIScience is both timely and imperative—shaping how we frame geospatial questions, choose data and algorithms, interpret results, and anticipate misuse.

In this symposium, we have multiple sessions broadly contributing to **human-centered and responsible GIScience**, with emphasis on understanding human–environment interactions, promoting quality of life, and prioritizing human values in geospatial data science.

---

## Call for Participation

If you have questions, please contact:
- Lei Zou (lzou@tamu.edu)  
- Yuhao Kang (yuhao.kang@austin.utexas.edu)

---

## Topics of Interest (examples)

We welcome topics including, but not limited to:

- Advanced GIScience methods (e.g., GeoAI, Spatial Data Science, Digital Twins) for modeling human–environment relationships  
- Human subjective experiences (emotion, perception, cognition) across places and scales  
- Human behavior, mobility, and spatiotemporal dynamics  
- Creativity in cartography, urban design, and geovisualization  
- Responsible/ethical GIScience, bias mitigation, and geoprivacy  
- GIScience applications for public health, hazards, housing, crime, transportation, and social good  
- AI literacy in teaching and learning

---

## Planned Sessions

Below is the current list of planned sessions for the GISER symposium. Session details (format, speakers, and timing) will be updated as they are confirmed.

## Planned Sessions

- **Multimodal Human Mobility** (Led by Luyu Liu)  
- **Past, Present, and Future of GIScience (Panel)** (Led by Dr. Lei Zou)  
- **AI & Ethics in Geography Education** (Led by Dr. Yue Lin)  
- **Urban Environmental Challenges and Mobility Dynamics** (Led by Dr. Suyeon Hwang)  
- **Less Is More? The Value of Small Geospatial Data in the Era of Big Data and AI (Panel)** (Led by Dr. Lei Zou)  
- **Humans in GIScience and GeoAI (Panel)** (Led by Dr. Shih-Lung Shaw)  
- **Integration of AI and GIS for Smart Urban Management: Opportunities, Challenges, Education, and Workforce Development** (Led by Dr. Junghwan Kim)  
- **GIScience Advances in Climate Effects on Environmental Health** (Led by Joynal Abedin)  
- **Sensing Human Experiences with Emerging Geospatial Methods I** (Led by Dr. Yuhao Kang)  
- **Ethics in GIScience (Panel)** (Led by Dr. Hongyu Zhang)  
- **Sensing Human Experiences with Emerging Geospatial Methods II** (Led by Dr. Jiaxin Feng)  
- **Sensing Urban Human Dynamics Through Multi-Source Insights (Part I)** (Led by Dr. Hao Tian)  
- **Sensing Human Experiences with Emerging Geospatial Methods III** (Led by Dr. Jiaxin Feng)  
- **GIScience and Hazards I** (Led by Dr. Bandana Kar)  
- **Sensing Human Experiences with Emerging Geospatial Methods IV** (Led by Dr. Jiaxin Feng)  
- **Sensing Urban Human Dynamics Through Multi-Source Insights (Part II)** (Led by Dr. Heng Cai)  
- **Using Geospatial Technologies to Promote Human-Centered Urban Sustainability** (Led by Dr. Zhang Chen)  
- **Human-Centered Assessments of Disaster Risk and Resilience** (Led by Dr. Md Zakaria Salim)  
- **GIScience and Hazards II** (Led by Dr. Bandana Kar)  
- **Linking Human Physiology and Perception of Space** (Led by Armita Kar)  
- **Human Networks and Data Science (Panel)** (Led by Dr. Lei Zou)  
- **Human-Environment Interactions in Digital Twin and 3D Urban Space** (Led by Zongrong Li)  
- **Future of Human-Centered Geospatial AI (Panel)** (Led by Dr. Jiaxin Feng)  
- **Sensing Urban Mobility Experiences for Human-Centered Learning and Decision-Making** (Led by Armita Kar)  
- **Crime Analysis and Public Safety 1 (Panel)** (Led by Hanlin Zhou)  
- **Crime Analysis and Public Safety 2** (Led by Hanlin Zhou)

- **Crime Analysis and Public Safety 3** (Led by Hanlin Zhou)  
- **Disability GIS** (Led by Shiloh Deitz)
